post.content: [Link to my other designs](https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/system-design?currentPage=1&orderBy=most_relevant&query=_spook_) **Disclaimer- None of my designs are complete and they do not address all the concerns, corner cases. Especially they might lack the sophiscated completeness of fault taulerance in the design. I created these designs with a clock timer of 40 minutes to practice because most companies has 40 min interviews.** Posting my Uber System Design
Is this level of detail expected for system design in general?
Great set of posts. Which app / software are you using for drawing the diagrams and text? Thanks.
Posting this link so this image can be better viewed-\nhttps://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Z5DQI2CKBC_zmRbAYIOS8Qvapmj9mfn/view?usp=sharing
can you share your other design link?
Hi, thank you for your post. is this infra or produce design
Would you use kafka for message bus here?

My UBER SYSTEM DESIGN: 

FR: 
1) Show a list of drivers nearby my location 
2) Book a ride 
3) Track the location of the driver and the rider 
4) Show the ETA (Estimated time of arrival)
5) Pricing - Real time 
6) Engagement: Reviews and ratings 



NR: 
1) Availablity & Reliable 
2) Scalability 
3) Fault tolerant 
4) Eventual Consistency 


Capacity Estimation: 

No. of active users per day = 2 Million 
No. of active drivers = 60K 
No. of rides per day = 800K 

Total number of drivers = 2 Million 

Time take to notify the location = 4 seconds 
Size of driver data = 40bytes 

Driver: 
d_id  d_name total_trips 

Total driver storage = 2*10^6 * 40 = 80 MB 

Bandwidth Estimate: 
2 Million * 20 =40 MB per 4 sec = 10 Mbps 


Data Models: 
Driver 
User 

Driver: 
d_id          d_name        contact_no    location       availability_status  trip_details 


User: 
username      password      loctaion       contact_no      trip_count       trip_details 


trip: 
trip_id      d_id    uId    s_loc   d_loc     price       start_time       reach_time   distance  rating reviews 




High Level Design: 
Concepts to be understood before HLD: 
** Geospatial Database: 
    --> stores, queries and indexes the spatial data 
** Geospatial Indexing: 
    --> These are index that supports containment and intersection queries for 
        various geometric shapes that are in 2D.
** Quadtree Indexing:
